title: Count loop length in Linked List

content.problemStatement:
Given a linked list, there may exist a single loop or no loop. If there are a total of **X** elements present in a loop, return **X-1** as your answer; otherwise, in case no loop exists, return **-1**.

To solve this problem, complete the function present in the submit solution tab.


Input Format

The first line will contain N, representing the elements in the linked list.

(For example, if N equals 5, the linked list looks like 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5)



The second line contains M, representing the last node's connection point. If the second line contains -1, then the last node will be connected to a null node; otherwise, it will be connected to one of the previous nodes.

(Example 1: If M equals -1 and N equals 5, the linked list looks like 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> NULL)


(Example 2: If M equals 2 and N equals 5, the linked list looks like 1 -> 2 -> 3 -> 4 -> 5 -> 2)
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of a line with two integers N and M separated by a space. N is the number of nodes in the linked list (1 ≤ N ≤ 10^5). M is the position (1‑based) to which the last node points; M = -1 means the last node points to null, otherwise it creates a single loop.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer on its own line: the length of the loop minus one, or -1 if no loop exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The list 1→2→3→4→5 ends at null, so there is no loop. The answer is -1.
The last node (5) points to node 2, forming a cycle 2→3→4→5 of length 4. Returned value is 4‑1 = 3.
Node 10 points to node 5, creating a cycle 5→6→7→8→9→10 of length 6. Output is 6‑1 = 5.

solution.approach: Use Floyd's cycle‑finding algorithm (slow and fast pointers). If the fast pointer meets the slow pointer, a loop exists. To obtain the loop length, keep the slow pointer fixed and move another pointer around the cycle counting steps until it returns to the meeting node. The required answer is (loop length – 1). If the fast pointer reaches null, there is no loop and we output -1. The construction of the list follows the given N and M values.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(N) total for all test cases combined.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (the list nodes themselves are required by the problem).

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{static class Node{int val;Node next;Node(int v){val=v;}}static int loopLengthMinusOne(Node head){Node slow=head,fast=head;while(fast!=null&&fast.next!=null){slow=slow.next;fast=fast.next.next;if(slow==fast){int len=1;Node cur=slow.next;while(cur!=slow){len++;cur=cur.next;}return len-1;}}return -1;}public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());while(T-- >0){StringTokenizer st=new StringTokenizer(br.readLine());int N=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());int M=Integer.parseInt(st.nextToken());Node head=null,tail=null;for(int i=1;i<=N;i++){Node nd=new Node(i);if(head==null)head=tail=nd;else{tail.next=nd;tail=nd;}}if(M!=-1){Node target=head;for(int i=1;i<M;i++)target=target.next;tail.next=target;}sb.append(loopLengthMinusOne(head)).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
